markets.question: Monnet vs. Jones: Match O/U 21.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Carole Monnet and Francesca Jones in the Istanbul, originally scheduled for May 6, 2026 at 4:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed across all sets equals or exceeds 22. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
All tiebreaks—including any Champions/Super tiebreak—count as one (1) game toward the total.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official WTA Challenger statistics.
markets.groupItemTitle: Istanbul: Carole Monnet vs Francesca Jones Match O/U 21.5

events.title: Istanbul: Carole Monnet vs Francesca Jones
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Carole Monnet and Francesca Jones in the Istanbul, originally scheduled for May 6, 2026 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Carole Monnet' if Carole Monnet advances against Francesca Jones.

This market will resolve to 'Francesca Jones' if Francesca Jones advances against Carole Monnet.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the WTA 125 Istanbul Open on outdoor clay, second seed Francesca Jones (No. 101) holds trader consensus edge over Carole Monnet (No. 181) in this round-of-16 matchup, buoyed by her straight-sets first-round win over Ekaterine Gorgodze (6-4, 6-2) two days ago and a dominant 6-1, 6-2 head-to-head clay-court victory over Monnet last July in Contrexeville. Monnet advanced via a gritty 6-4, 7-6(3) tiebreak triumph against Maria Sara Popa, leveraging her left-handed game, but Jones' superior ranking, seeding, and 11 career clay titles underscore her baseline consistency and recent form amid the European clay swing. No injury concerns reported; expect a tactical battle testing Monnet's resilience against Jones' experience.

markets.question: Bab el-Mandeb Strait effectively closed by September 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF PortWatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Bab el-Mandeb Strait less than or equal to 10 for any date between market creation and the listed date. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF PortWatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls for the Bab el-Mandeb Strait equal to or below 10, or once data has been published for the listed date and no such value has been published.

If no data has been published for the listed date within 14 calendar days (ET) after that date, this market will resolve based on the data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made before data has been published for the listed date will be considered; however, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions made after data has been published for the listed date will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF PortWatch, specifically the “Arrivals of Ships” data published for the Bab el-Mandeb Strait at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/6b1814d64903461b98144a6cc25eb79c.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket's Bab el-Mandeb Strait closure market reflects low near-term risks despite Houthi threats, pricing May 31 at 6.7% implied probability and September 30 leading at around 17% amid $2.5 million volume, as IMF PortWatch ship arrivals hold at 30-50% of pre-crisis levels—well above the ≤10 threshold for resolution. Escalating Iran-Yemen tensions and US naval patrols have sustained disruptions, driving war risk insurance premiums to 0.2-1% of hull value and rerouting costs up $1 million per Asia-Europe voyage, boosting Baltic Dry Index to 2,991. Brent crude embeds a geopolitical premium near $90-115/barrel. Key catalysts: Houthi responses to Trump administration policies and weekly PortWatch updates.

markets.question: Londrina EC vs. São Bernardo FC: O/U 3.5
markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Londrina EC and São Bernardo FC combine to score 4 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 4, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on cbf.com.br. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

Head-to-head tilts heavily toward stalemates—three draws and one Londrina victory across four prior meetings, including 2025 Série C promotion playoff ties—bolstered by both teams' recent draw-prone form amid defensive solidity. markets.question: Will Zelenskyy post 80-99 posts from May 5 to May 12, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Volodymyr Zelenskyy (@ZelenskyyUa), posts on X between May 5, 12:00 PM ET and May 12, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects tightly clustered probabilities across Zelenskyy’s projected X post count for May 5-12, with <20 and 40-59 each at 27% amid recent weeks' output of 64 posts (April 17-24) and 60-79 (April 24-May 1), per resolved markets. eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a razor-thin 50% implied probability for Conner Huertas Del Pino in this ATP Challenger Santos clay-court second-round clash against second-seeded Franco Roncadelli, driven by their evenly split 1-1 head-to-head on clay—Roncadelli's 6-4, 7-6(5) edge in 2021 offsetting Huertas Del Pino's prior 6-3, 6-1 win—and comparable 2026 clay records (Roncadelli 14-9, Huertas Del Pino 11-10). markets.question: Game 1: Any Player Quadra Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. In League of Legends, a Quadra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Quadra Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Quadra Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: Ninjas in Pyjamas vs Anyone's Legend (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
events.description:

This market will resolve to "Ninjas in Pyjamas" if Ninjas in Pyjamas win the match against Anyone's Legend.

This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend win the match against Ninjas in Pyjamas.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anyone's Legend holds a commanding edge in trader consensus for this LPL Split 2 Group Ascend BO3 rematch, buoyed by their 7-3 record atop the standings versus Ninjas in Pyjamas' middling 4-5 mark and a decisive 2-0 head-to-head win on April 22 where jungler Tarzan earned MVP honors in both games. AL's stable roster—Flandre top, Tarzan jungle, Shanks mid, Hope ADC, Kael support—has delivered consistent macro execution in the double round-robin format, climbing to second place with strong early-game pressure. NIP's revamped lineup featuring Korean imports Hoya top and Guwon jungle shows upset potential after sweeping JD Gaming, but recent losses like 0-2 to Top Esports highlight adaptation struggles ahead of playoffs qualification battles.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Destroy Inhibitors?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 1. In League of Legends, each team has three inhibitors (one in each lane), located behind the inner turrets.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Ninjas in Pyjamas and Anyone's Legend each destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) do not destroy any enemy inhibitors.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams destroyed at least one inhibitor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: SC Freiburg host SC Braga in the UEFA Europa League semi-final second leg on May 7 at Europa-Park Stadion, following the first leg in Portugal on April 30. Freiburg, 7th in the Bundesliga with a good overall record of 12 wins from 30 games, endured a shocking collapse against Bayern Munich on April 4 but advanced past quarter-final foes via resilience. Braga sit 4th in Primeira Liga, fresh off a 1-0 win at Casa Pia, though defender Sikou Niakaté (Achilles rupture) and midfielder Diego Rodrigues (ankle, early May) remain sidelined—offset by Rodrigo Zalazar's return. Freiburg miss midfielder Patrick Osterhage (knee surgery); no prior head-to-head heightens uncertainty in this evenly matched knockout tie.

If the 2026 MLB season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Paul Skenes tops trader consensus at 41% implied probability to repeat as NL Cy Young winner, bolstered by his defending 2025 title and elite early 2026 form—5-2 record, 2.36 ERA, 46 strikeouts over eight starts with a 0.58 WHIP in his last seven outings for the Pirates. Cristopher Sanchez (10%) surges on Phillies' staff with 3-2, 2.42 ERA, and Chris Sale (8%) excels for Braves at 6-1, 2.14 ERA, 49 K's, reflecting a tight race driven by one-month-in sub-3.00 ERAs amid health and workload questions.

Dylan Cease (11%, Toronto) and Jose Soriano (10%, Angels) trail via high strikeouts and sub-3.00 ERAs, but face tougher run support and workload questions in a wide-open rotation landscape defined by first-month momentum.

markets.question: US x Cuba military clash in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military encounter between the military forces of the United States and Cuba between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military encounter" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between US and Cuban military forces. Non-violent actions, such as warning shots, artillery fire into uninhabited areas, or missile launches that land in territorial waters or pass through airspace, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution. Intentional ship ramming that results in significant damage to (e.g., a hole in the hull) or the sinking of a military ship by another will count toward a "Yes" resolution, however minor damage (scrapes, dents) will not.

Note: the United States Coast Guard is considered part of the United States military, and the Cuban Border Guard is considered part of the Cuban military.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Pentagon contingency planning for potential operations against Cuba, reported in mid-April 2026, alongside new U.S. sanctions and President Trump's rhetorical threats like "Cuba is next," have driven trader consensus to price a military clash at 45% for the year, with "No" leading at 55%. Cuban leaders, including President Díaz-Canel, affirmed military readiness amid accusations of U.S. aggression, yet ongoing diplomatic visits—such as U.S. officials' April proposals for Havana reforms—and absence of deployments signal de-escalation paths. markets.question: Will Michael Kim win the 2026 Truist Championship?

This market will resolve according to the listed player who wins the 2026 Truist Championship tournament.

markets.question: Will Valtteri Bottas be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kimi Antonelli's three consecutive Grand Prix victories—capped by his Miami pole-to-win masterclass on May 3, including daring passes on Lando Norris and Max Verstappen—have propelled the 19-year-old Mercedes rookie to a 20-point Drivers' Championship lead with 100 points after four rounds, fueling trader consensus at 39% implied probability for the title. Teammate George Russell's consistent podiums and P4 in Miami keep him close at 80 points and 28% odds, underscoring Mercedes' constructors' dominance with 180 points amid Red Bull's struggles that have dropped Verstappen to seventh (26 points, 6% odds). Norris holds third overall (51 points, 11% odds) on McLaren momentum, but the silver cars' early-season edge drives the market's Mercedes focus.

markets.question: Will Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) hit (LOW) $540 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.META%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.META%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Map 2: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 2, or if Map 2 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Imperial Academy vs Infinite (BO3) - United21 Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Upper bracket quarterfinal 1 match between Imperial Academy and Infinite in the United21 Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 9 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Imperial Academy" if Imperial Academy win the match against Infinite.

This market will resolve to "Infinite" if Infinite win the match against Imperial Academy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Infinite entered the United21 Season 47 playoffs upper bracket quarterfinal BO3 against Imperial Academy as the clear trader consensus favorite, backed by their #59 HLTV world ranking, a dominant United21 Season 46 title, and 71% win rate across recent tournaments like Parken Challenger and Esplay Elite, driven by standouts sl3nd (1.27 rating) and kreaz. Imperial Academy, a young academy roster at #193 with 50% recent win rate, impressed by topping Group A undefeated (2-0, +16 round differential over LPH and Sangal ALTERS), showcasing map control on Inferno and Ancient. No recent head-to-head or roster changes; Infinite's form edged sentiment despite Imperial's group momentum, in a double-elimination playoff format resolving on series win.

markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Khamenei (@khamenei_ir), posts on X between May 5, 12:00 PM ET and May 12, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

This irregular pattern—bursts around events like Easter (multiple posts April 5) versus prolonged silences post-Ali Khamenei's March martyrdom—keeps probabilities tightly clustered near 25–27% across mid-to-high ranges, including a slight edge for 45–49. Escalation in regional diplomacy, sanctions, or commemorative dates could spur archival statements and boost higher bins; sustained internal leadership transitions or health concerns for Mojtaba might entrench low output below 10.

markets.question: Will Trump reduce the deficit before 2027?
markets.description:
The U.S. federal budget deficit for September 2025 was $197,949,630,362.16 (see: https://fiscaldata.treasury.gov/datasets/monthly-treasury-statement/summary-of-receipts-outlays-and-the-deficit-surplus-of-the-u-s-government). 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Monthly Treasury Statement (MTS) reports a lower monthly deficit in December 2026 than in September 2025. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source will be the Monthly Treasury Statement (MTS) published by the U.S. Department of the Treasury (fiscaldata.treasury.gov). The month surplus can be found in the column labeled "Current Month Deficit Surplus Amount" in the the table "Summary of Receipts, Outlays, and Surplus or Deficit” in the MTS (see: https://fiscaldata.treasury.gov/datasets/monthly-treasury-statement/summary-of-receipts-outlays-and-the-deficit-surplus-of-the-u-s-government).  If no report is published by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET another credible source will be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects an 84.5% implied probability for "No," driven by Congressional Budget Office projections showing the fiscal year 2026 federal deficit at $1.9 trillion (5.8% of GDP), with year-to-date figures already exceeding $1 trillion through February amid persistent revenue shortfalls and spending pressures. President Trump's policy mix—extension of 2017 tax cuts adding $1.4 trillion to decade-long deficits, alongside 42% defense spending hikes to $1.5 trillion in the April FY2027 budget proposal—offsets proposed non-defense discretionary cuts, per CBO analysis. Recent tariff policy shifts could further widen shortfalls by $1.1 trillion over ten years, reinforcing fiscal expansion. Key catalysts include final FY2026 Treasury data by September and debt ceiling negotiations.

markets.question: Estudiantes de La Plata vs. Racing Club: O/U 3.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Primera División Argentina game between Estudiantes de La Plata and Racing Club, scheduled for May 10 at 4:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Estudiantes de La Plata and Racing Club combine to score 4 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 4, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on afa.com.ar. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Estudiantes de La Plata vs. Racing Club - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Primera División Argentina game, scheduled for May 10 at 4:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Estudiantes de La Plata enters this Liga Profesional Argentina Apertura Round of 16 matchup atop Group A with 31 points from 16 games and a +12 goal difference, bolstered by home advantage at Estadio Ciudad de La Plata and an unbeaten streak in recent head-to-heads against Racing Club, including a 1-1 draw and multiple wins over the past year. Racing Club, sitting 8th in Group B on 21 points, advanced via a gritty 0-0 draw with Huracán but faces major setbacks: forward Ezequiel Cannavo sidelined by a right rectus anterior muscle tear from that game, midfielder Baltasar Rodríguez suspended for a fifth yellow, and defender Gastón Martirena out with an acromioclavicular sprain. These absences compound Racing's mid-table form, tilting trader consensus toward the hosts amid playoff intensity.

markets.question: Will Discord’s market cap be $30B or greater at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on Discord's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no IPO occurs by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO by June 30, 2026".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on Discord’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

Secondary market valuations have halved to $7-10 billion since 2021 peaks near $15 billion, amid SaaS multiple compression and profitability headwinds despite 200M+ monthly active users and ~$600-800 million annual recurring revenue. The 13.4% odds for a sub-$15 billion market cap reflect these depressed fundamentals and gamer-centric monetization challenges versus enterprise peers like Slack. No fresh catalysts have emerged in the past 30 days, embedding delay risks ahead of potential Q3 regulatory milestones.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the IL-09 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the IL-09 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Illinois' 9th Congressional District, with a Cook Partisan Voting Index of D+19, remains a stronghold for Democrats following longtime Rep. Jan Schakowsky's 2025 retirement announcement, which triggered a crowded primary resolved on March 17, 2026, when Evanston Mayor Daniel Biss secured the nomination over 15 challengers. Trader consensus at 92.5% for the Democratic Party reflects the district's consistent double-digit Democratic margins in recent cycles—Biden carried it by over 30 points in 2020—and Biss's fundraising edge heading into the November 3 general election against Republican nominee John Elleson. While a national Republican wave, major scandal involving Biss, or depressed Democratic turnout could challenge this outlook, historical base rates for such safe seats favor the party hold barring extraordinary developments.

eventMetadata.context_description: Early in the 2026 MLS season, trader consensus prices Pedro Gallese, Jonathan Sirois, Novak Mićović, and Daniel as virtual co-favorites around 50% implied probability, reflecting statistical parity across key metrics like sub-1.00 GAA, 75-85% save percentages, and 4-6 clean sheets apiece through 10-12 matches. Gallese has anchored Orlando City's stingy defense with precise distribution and clutch stops, while Sirois racks up high-volume saves for CF Montréal amid defensive lapses; Mićović's breakout shot-stopping has propelled Chicago Fire's rise, and Daniel's exceptional shutout rate for San Jose Earthquakes stands out despite modest minutes. No major injuries or slumps have emerged in recent matchdays, including standout penalty saves and clean sheets last week, sustaining the razor-thin competitive dynamics.

markets.question: Will annual inflation increase by 3.3% in April?
markets.description:
This is a market about inflation over the 12-month period ending April 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the Bureau of Labor Statistics.

This market will resolve to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the 12-month period ending in April 2026 according to the monthly Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) report.

The resolution source for this market will be the BLS Consumer Price Index report released for April 2026 (https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/cpi.htm), currently scheduled to be released on May 12, 2026, at 8:30 AM ET. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly BLS CPI news release, which reports inflation over 12-month periods to only one decimal point (e.g., 2.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

If the BLS does not release the relevant figures on the scheduled date, this market may remain open up until the scheduled release time of the next CPI report (https://www.bls.gov/schedule). If the information is not released by that time, this market will resolve according to the figures of the most recent previous month with available data.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price a tight contest for April 2026 CPI YoY at 3.7% (40.1% implied probability) and 3.8% (32.5%), reflecting uncertainty after March's headline inflation accelerated to 3.3%—the highest since May 2024—from 2.4%, driven by a 12.5% energy index surge amid global supply risks highlighted by Chevron's CEO. Elevated gasoline at $4.26/gallon in April, Brent crude peaking near $126, and base effects push headline expectations higher, while core CPI's modest 2.6% rise and ongoing shelter disinflation temper broader reacceleration fears. Consensus forecasts cluster near 3.6-3.7%, with swing factors including food price stability and final energy passthrough ahead of the May 12 BLS release.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Tokyo be 24°C on May 9?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Tokyo Haneda Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 9 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Tokyo Haneda Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/jp/tokyo/RJTT.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

events.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Halim Perdanakusuma Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 8 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Halim Perdanakusuma Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/id/jakarta/WIHH.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket closely splits between 34°C and 35°C for Jakarta's highest temperature on May 8, driven by the latest BMKG forecast update on May 7 indicating berawan (overcast) conditions across DKI Jakarta with maximums of 30-32°C at stations including East Jakarta's Halim Perdanakusuma Airport—the market's resolution source—tempered by 64-96% humidity and light winds. This scientific uncertainty stems from potential afternoon cloud breaks amid the dry season onset, allowing greater solar insolation and urban heat island effects to push peaks higher, as seen in early May actuals reaching 35°C+ nearby. Historical May averages at Halim hover near 33°C, but model ensembles diverge on sea breeze timing, which moderates or amplifies heating; final observations will resolve by May 9.

markets.question: Will José Ignacio García be the next President of Andalusia following the regional election?
markets.description:
Elections for the autonomous community of Andalusia’s unicameral parliament are scheduled for May 17, 2026. The President of Andalusia will then be elected by the Parliament of Andalusia elected in this election.

This market will resolve according to the first individual elected by the Parliament of Andalusia to serve as President of Andalusia following this election.

Interim, temporary, or caretaker Presidents will not count.

If no such President has been elected by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of Andalusia; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Juanma Moreno of the Partido Popular commands overwhelming trader consensus at 98% implied probability to remain President of the Junta de Andalucía following the May 17 regional election, driven by consistent polling averages over the past month showing his party securing an absolute majority of over 55 seats in the 109-seat Parliament of Andalusia. Recent surveys from Sigma Dos, Target Point, and CIS highlight Moreno's high approval as the top-valued leader, bolstered by vote gains from a declining Vox and stagnant PSOE under candidate María Jesús Montero, amid a campaign kickoff marked by debates and focus on housing and immigration. While structural incumbency and historical precedent favor continuity from his 2022 absolute majority win, low-probability shifts could arise from late scandals, voter turnout surges, or post-election coalition dynamics if polls tighten unexpectedly.

markets.question: Will Tarik Skubal strike out the most batters during the 2026 MLB regular season?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the pitcher who records the most strikeouts among pitchers during the 2026 Major League Baseball regular season.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by the rules of the MLB. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the pitcher that records fewer innings pitched during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve in favor of the pitcher that records the lower ERA during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, then this market will resolve in favor of the pitcher that walked fewer batters during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve to the pitcher whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLB regular season is cancelled, postponed after October 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from MLB; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Tsitsipas vs. Machac: Set 1 Games O/U 9.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Stefanos Tsitsipas and Tomas Machac in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 6, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed in the first set equals or exceeds 10. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
Any first-set tiebreak counts as one (1) game toward the total.
If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins, or delayed beyond 7 days without commencement, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official ATP results.

events.title: Internazionali BNL d'Italia: Stefanos Tsitsipas vs Tomas Machac
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Stefanos Tsitsipas and Tomas Machac in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 6, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Stefanos Tsitsipas' if Stefanos Tsitsipas advances against Tomas Machac.

This market will resolve to 'Tomas Machac' if Tomas Machac advances against Stefanos Tsitsipas.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Stefanos Tsitsipas faces Tomas Machac in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia first round on the slow clay of Rome's Foro Italico, their third meeting and first on this surface after a 1-1 head-to-head—Tsisipas won in Vienna 2023, Machac prevailed in five sets at the 2026 Australian Open. Tsitsipas arrives with momentum from a convincing 6-2, 7-5 upset of Alexander Bublik to reach Madrid's third round, rebounding from an early Monte-Carlo loss amid a 13-9 season, while leveraging his past Rome finalist pedigree. Machac posts a steady 12-9 record with a title, but showed vulnerability in Madrid; rain delays add uncertainty, though no injuries are reported.

markets.question: Will Claudia López win the 2026 Colombian presidential election?
markets.description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election. 

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Candidate M a narrow edge at 49.5% to win Colombia's presidential election, with Iván Cepeda Castro close at 41.5% and Abelardo de la Espriella at 37.5%, reflecting a tight three-way first-round race ahead of the May 31 vote and potential June runoff. Recent Invamer and CNC polls (late April-early May) show Cepeda leading first-round voting intention at 37-44%, but right-wing fragmentation between de la Espriella and Paloma Valencia (15-23%) prevents separation, bolstered by the left's strong March congressional showing for Pacto Histórico. Rising armed group violence heightens turnout uncertainty, while runoff scenarios favor right consolidation. Debates, endorsements, or scandals could tip the balance for vote unification.

markets.question: Will Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) hit (LOW) $4.00 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.OPEN%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.OPEN%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

Alavés boast no major absences but face stylistic challenges against Barça's possession dominance, as seen in Barcelona's comeback 3-1 win in November; end-of-season rotation and fatigue could sway dynamics.

markets.question: Will between 13 and 15 albums be ranked #1 on the Billboard 200 in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of albums that are ranked number 1 on any official weekly Billboard 200 albums chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026.

Each specific album may only count once, regardless of the number times it reaches the number 1 spot. Different editions of the same album will be considered to be different specific albums if they contain different tracklists.

Billboard updates the Billboard 200 albums chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data from the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard 200 album chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard 200 album chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

All officially published Billboard 200 Albums charts will be treated as final. Revisions made after the release of a Billboard 200 album chart will not be considered.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official weekly Billboard 200 albums chart, published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/billboard-200/ and through other official Billboard channels; however, a consensus of credible reporting on albums that reached number 1 on the Billboard 200 within this market's timeframe may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket closely splits between 19-21 (41.9%) and 25+ (41.0%) albums topping the Billboard 200 in 2026, reflecting the year's blistering pace of 14 unique No. 1 debuts in the first 19 chart weeks through May 9, driven by powerhouse releases from Taylor Swift, Zach Bryan, J. Cole, Bad Bunny, Bruno Mars, Harry Styles, BTS's three-week Arirang run—the longest yet—and Noah Kahan's recent Great Divide bow. High streaming units and vinyl sales for genre-spanning acts like country, rap, K-pop, and pop have fueled weekly turnover, outpacing historical norms where recent years saw 20-30 crowns. Key swings hinge on second-half schedules crowded with Drake's Iceman, Kendrick Lamar projects, and others; sustained debuts could push toward 25+, while holdovers like Wallen's I'm the Problem might cap at 19-21. Next charts and Luminate data will clarify momentum.

markets.question: Will there be no change in Fed interest rates after the September 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal funds range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's September 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for September 15-16, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their September meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a strong inclination toward no Federal Reserve rate change at the September 15-16, 2026 FOMC meeting, driven by persistent inflation above the 2% target and resilient labor market conditions. The Fed held the federal funds target range steady at 3.50%-3.75% following its April 28-29 decision—Chair Powell's last before his May 15 term ends—with March CPI accelerating to 3.3% year-over-year and unemployment steady at 4.3% amid 178,000 nonfarm payroll gains. Markets price limited near-term easing via CME FedWatch, prioritizing data-dependent policy amid nominee Kevin Warsh's impending confirmation. Watch April CPI release on May 12 and June 16-17 FOMC for potential shifts in rate expectations.

markets.question: Fed rate hike in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is increased at any point between January 1, 2026 and the Fed's December 2026 meeting, currently scheduled for December 8-9, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the Fed has released its rate change decision following its December meeting.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will Xi Jinping win the Nobel Peace Prize in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize, as announced by the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

If multiple listed individuals or entities jointly receive the prize, the market will resolve to a single winner. If Donald Trump, Volodymyr Zelenskyy,  Benjamin Netanyahu, Vladimir Putin, or Elon Musk are among the recipients, the market will resolve to the highest-ranked among them in that exact order of precedence. If none of those five are among the winners and the prize is awarded jointly to at least one listed individual and at least one listed organization, the market will resolve in favor of the individual. If no prioritized individuals are among the winners and all listed recipients are of the same type (all individuals or all organizations), the market will resolve to the person whose last name, or the entity’s name, comes first in alphabetical order as listed in this market.

If no official announcement regarding the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize has been made by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other."

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement from the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

Donald Trump trails closely at 6.5%, propelled by reported nominations from leaders in Cambodia, Israel, and Pakistan highlighting his deal-making narrative, while Volodymyr Zelenskyy's 6.0% stems from his nomination alongside Ukraine's people for wartime resilience. No outcome exceeds 8%, underscoring competitive dynamics driven by geopolitical narratives, secret committee deliberations, and potential late momentum from peace breakthroughs; traders eye October 9 announcement amid high uncertainty in this precedent-defying race.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If Game 1 is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. 

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50.

events.title: LoL: Kiwoom DRX vs Gen.G (BO3) - LCK Rounds 1-2
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Kiwoom DRX and Gen.G in the LCK Rounds 1-2, initially scheduled for May 8 at 6:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Kiwoom DRX" if Kiwoom DRX win the match against Gen.G.

This market will resolve to "Gen.G" if Gen.G win the match against Kiwoom DRX.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gen.G commands trader consensus as the clear favorite in this LCK Rounds 1-2 BO3 matchup against Kiwoom DRX, buoyed by their 3rd-place standing in Split 2 behind dominant HLE and KT, contrasted with DRX's mid-table struggles. The April 30 Week 5 clash, resolved just two days ago with Gen.G's 2-1 win, underscores the gap—DRX took early games competitively, but Gen.G's macro prowess shone in a chaotic Game 3 decided by minion waves. Recent form favors Gen.G, including upsets over DK, bolstered by Chovy's midlane dominance and Peyz's jungle aggression; DRX's roster inconsistencies persist amid current meta emphasizing teamfight scaling, with no major injury news or patch shifts altering dynamics ahead of potential rematches.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Republican Party to contest the GA-08 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Republican primary will take place on May 19, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican sources, including https://rnc.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Rep. Austin Scott commands 93% trader consensus in the GA-08 Republican primary due to his 15-year tenure in the solidly Republican district (R+15 Cook PVI), history of unopposed or dominant primaries—including 2024—and superior fundraising with over $1.3 million cash on hand as of late March. Challenger Vinson Watkins, a perennial low-fundraiser who raised just $5,000 in his 2024 Democratic primary bid here (losing 28%-72%), trails at 4.7% amid reports of his withdrawal or disqualification, leaving Scott effectively unopposed ahead of the May 19 primary. Realistic challenges would require a late scandal, health issue, or procedural reversal reinstating Watkins with unexpected turnout, though historical incumbent re-election rates exceed 90% in safe seats.

markets.question: Will OpenAI announce a tablet in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to "Yes" if the listed type of consumer hardware product is publicly announced by OpenAI by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The announcement of a qualifying consumer hardware product within the market timeframe will suffice, regardless of whether or when the product is released.

If a device fulfills multiple device categories, then all relevant categories will resolve to "Yes".

The resolution source will be official information from OpenAI.

Recent supply chain leaks, including May reports of a MediaTek-powered AI agent smartphone targeting 2027 production, have boosted phone odds, while persistent rumors of earbuds or clip-on wearables explain the fragmented sentiment across categories. No qualifying hardware has materialized amid April-May software releases like GPT-5.5, underscoring execution risks; watch OpenAI DevDay on September 29 for potential reveals amid competitive pressure from Meta and Apple AR pushes.

Fresh Mexico national team call-ups announced April 28 spare Tigres any absences while sidelining five Chivas players, including goalkeeper Raúl Rangel and Roberto Alvarado, though coach Gabriel Milito confirmed returns for José Castillo and Ángel Sepúlveda. Tigres monitor lingering injuries to Marcelo Flores and Vladimir Loroña, but their depth positions them competitively in this high-stakes opener.

eventMetadata.context_description: Paul Skenes tops trader consensus at 41% implied probability to repeat as NL Cy Young winner, bolstered by his defending 2025 title and elite early 2026 form—5-2 record, 2.36 ERA, 46 strikeouts over eight starts with a 0.58 WHIP in his last seven outings for the Pirates. markets.question: Games Total: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 3 match between MOUZ NXT and Lavked in the CCT Europe Series 1 Group Stage, initially scheduled for May 7 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if MOUZ NXT and Lavked play 3 or more maps in this series.

If fewer than 3 maps are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: Counter-Strike: MOUZ NXT vs Lavked (BO3) - CCT Europe Series 1 Group Stage
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 3 match between MOUZ NXT and Lavked in the CCT Europe Series 1 Group Stage, initially scheduled for May 7 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "MOUZ NXT" if MOUZ NXT win the match against Lavked.

This market will resolve to "Lavked" if Lavked win the match against MOUZ NXT.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the CCT 2026 Europe Series 1 Swiss group stage round 3 BO3 matchup, both MOUZ NXT and Lavked enter at 1-1 records, with a win advancing toward the $25,000 playoffs amid single-elimination pressure. Lavked (#65 HLTV) boasts a superior 65% win rate over the past three months and standout performers like Djon8 (1.19 rating), following a 2-1 upset over Omega but a 0-2 loss to TNC. MOUZ NXT (#139 HLTV), the MOUZ academy squad with stable roster including opdust and Nikodeon, notched a forfeit win vs. Bebop after a 1-2 defeat to Ursa, amid a 50% win rate and four straight competitive losses prior. Map veto looms key, favoring Lavked on Ancient (79% win rate) and Mirage (69%), while both split evenly on Dust2; no recent roster changes or patches alter the meta.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spirit Academy, Team Spirit's academy squad with a February 2026 roster refresh including rifler Kiryasoo and coach S0tF1k, enters this BO3 Exort Series #25 Main Stage matchup as the pedigree favorite against HLTV #251-ranked Wave Esports, whose March lineup additions like Belof and Katalic have yielded a 37.5% win rate over recent maps. eventMetadata.context_description: Aryna Sabalenka leads trader consensus as world No. 1 with just two losses in 2026, bolstering her implied probability despite a gritty quarterfinal exit to Hailey Baptiste at the Mutua Madrid Open last week. Iga Świątek, traditionally dominant on Roland Garros clay with multiple titles, has faltered in her 2026 clay-court swing, suffering an early Stuttgart quarterfinal upset to Mirra Andreeva and a retirement against Ann Li in Madrid, dropping her to No. 4 in WTA rankings. Elena Rybakina's strong indoor-clay Stuttgart final run elevates her standing, while Coco Gauff holds as defending champion amid solid form, and surging teen Andreeva gains from her Madrid runner-up finish over Kostyuk after toppling Świątek. Rome's early rounds loom as the final clay test before the Grand Slam.

eventMetadata.context_description: Plaid Cymru leader Rhun ap Iorwerth commands 98.7% trader consensus as next First Minister following yesterday's final YouGov MRP poll projecting his party as largest in the Senedd election with 37 seats, ahead of Reform UK (28) and a collapsing Welsh Labour (under incumbent Eluned Morgan) at historic lows. Months of voter discontent over NHS waiting lists, economic stagnation, and devolution tensions with Westminster have driven Plaid's surge, reflected in consistent polling leads through April. The Senedd convenes post-results for a confidence vote to select the FM under the new 96-member proportional system. A dramatic Reform plurality or unexpected centre-left coalition excluding Plaid could challenge this, though alternatives like Dan Thomas (Reform) or Jane Dodds (Lib Dems) trail far behind.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Istanbul be 19°C on May 8?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by NOAA at the Istanbul Airport in degrees Celsius on 8 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from NOAA, specifically the highest reading under the "Temp" column on the specified date once information is finalized for all hours on that date, available here: https://www.weather.gov/wrh/timeseries?site=LTFM

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the "Switch to Metric Units" button until the relevant table displays °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest ensemble forecasts from the European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF) and Global Forecast System (GFS), corroborated by Turkish State Meteorological Service (MGM) guidance, project Istanbul's highest temperature on May 8 at around 24°C under partly cloudy to overcast skies, positioning "24°C or higher" as the market-implied leader at 65.5% probability. This reflects a rapid warming trend following a cold snap earlier in the week, where highs dipped to the mid-teens amid northerly winds and frontal passages; current high-pressure ridging now enables southerly warm air advection. AccuWeather aligns with a 24°C peak, though model spreads introduce uncertainty around 23–25°C. Traders await 12z GFS and ECMWF updates later today, plus MGM observations from Istanbul Airport, the official measurement site, as resolution nears. Historical May averages hover near 21°C, underscoring the above-normal outlook.

events.description:
The MSI 2026 (Mid-Season Invitational) is scheduled to take place from June 26 to July 12, 2026.

This market will resolve according to which teams qualify for MSI 2026.

If the listed team officially qualifies as one of the teams invited to MSI 2026, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

Ties in standings will be broken according to the official LoL Esports rules.

If MSI 2026 is canceled, postponed indefinitely, or if the official list of MSI 2026 participants is not published before August 1, 2026, ET, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the LoL Esports website (https://lolesports.com). However, a consensus of credible reporting (e.g., Liquipedia at https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Mid-Season_Invitational/2026) may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hanwha Life Esports' dominant 10-1 regular season record in LCK Split 2 has solidified their frontrunner status for Road to MSI qualification, with KT Rolster (9-2) and Gen.G (8-3) vying for the second LCK spot amid recent upset wins boosting trader focus on Korean depth. Bilibili Gaming leads LPL Split 2 at 8-2 following key victories over TES and JDG last week, while Team Vitality's 8-1 LEC Spring series pace sets up a playoff clash with undefeated Karmine Corp. LCS remains tight with Team Liquid and Cloud9 both at 5-0 early, and LCP/GAM Esports surging. No teams locked for the June 28 Daejeon event yet—playoffs starting late May carry high upset potential via BO5 formats and meta adaptations.

markets.question: Will XRP dip to $1.00 May 4-10?
markets.description:
This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for XRP (XRP/USDT) during the date range specified in the title (from 12:00 AM ET on the first date to 11:59 PM ET on the last) has a final "Low" price equal to or lower than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the XRP/USDT "Low" prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/XRP_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance XRP/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

markets.question: Abstract FDV above $200M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Abstract's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Abstract doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will David Njoku play for Indianapolis Colts in 2026-27?
markets.description:

If David Njoku does not officially join a new team by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If David Njoku joins a team that is not listed, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official announcements from the NFL and/or the acquiring team. A consensus of credible media reporting may also be used to resolve the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Free agent tight end David Njoku, who announced in February his departure from the Cleveland Browns after nine seasons to seek a new home, remains unsigned post-NFL Draft, driving tight trader consensus across multiple landing spots for the 2026-27 season. The Los Angeles Chargers lead slightly at 49.3% implied probability following a recent visit reported by Ian Rapoport, edging out Bengals (44.4%) and Cardinals (43.5%) amid widespread TE needs; teams like Colts, Falcons, Eagles, Texans, and Chiefs cluster around 41-43% due to cap space, Justin Herbert/Herbert-like QB synergies, and schemes valuing receiving tight ends. Njoku's Pro Bowl production is tempered by injury history suppressing his market, while Browns odds lag at 24.5% per his explicit no-return stance, keeping the race competitive among a dozen viable contenders.

markets.question: O/U 2.5 Rounds
markets.description:
Each market will resolve to “Over” if the fight between Sean Strickland and Khamzat Chimaev at UFC 328: Chimaev vs. Strickland, scheduled for May 9, 2026, lasts beyond the listed round threshold.

Each market will resolve to “Under” if the fight between Sean Strickland and Khamzat Chimaev at UFC 328: Chimaev vs. Strickland, scheduled for May 9, 2026, does not last beyond the listed round threshold.

For example:

“Over 2.5 Rounds” will resolve “Yes” if the fight continues past the 2:30 mark of Round 3.

“Under 2.5 Rounds” will resolve “Yes” if the fight ends before the 2:30 mark of Round 3.

If the fight ends exactly at the threshold (e.g., 2:30 of Round 3 for a 2.5 line), it will resolve “50-50.”

If the bout is not scored, postponed beyond May 23, 2026, or canceled, all options will resolve “50-50.”

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC.
