Mild spring weather has limited power sector burn, keeping shoulder-season dynamics bearish. Traders eye today's EIA storage update (consensus +72 Bcf injection) and extended weather outlooks, with EIA projecting 2Q26 averages around $3.10/MMBtu amid rising summer power demand risks.

markets.question: Trump-Denmark Greenland deal signed by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if both Denmark and the United States sign a deal, treaty, or similar international agreement of any kind relating to Greenland by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any U.S.–Danish agreement relating to Greenland will qualify, regardless of subject matter, including but not limited to sovereignty, governance, security arrangements, or access to natural resources.

Examples of qualifying deals include but are not limited to a treaty that makes any portion of Greenland a U.S. territory or possession (even if the handover date for such territory or possession is later); or, a Guantánamo-style arrangement treaty establishing a defined zone in Greenland under exclusive or primary U.S. jurisdiction and control, where Denmark and Greenland’s ordinary legal authority does not apply except by U.S. permission; or agreements permitting additional U.S. troop stationing, basing access, or resource extraction rights in Greenland.

This market will resolve to “Yes” only if a qualifying agreement is formally signed by authorized representatives of both Denmark and the United States. Official announcements, statements of intent, or declarations that an agreement has been reached will not suffice unless accompanied by signatures from both sides. Whether or not a qualifying deal is later passed by the respective parliaments or enters into force will not affect this market’s resolution. Signaling from Greenland’s population will not be considered.

Announcements, negotiations, proposals, frameworks, or understandings that are not formally signed by both parties will not qualify. Any qualifying U.S. jurisdiction, control, basing rights, or access arrangements in Greenland that existed at market creation will not count as new qualifying agreements.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Denmark; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a 91.5% implied probability against a Trump-Denmark Greenland deal signed by June 30, driven by Denmark's and Greenland's unwavering insistence that sovereignty remains non-negotiable. Trump's January 2026 revival of acquisition ambitions—initially backed by tariff threats on NATO allies and military rhetoric—prompted massive protests in Nuuk, European solidarity with Copenhagen, and early Danish elections in March amid public backlash. While Davos talks led to plans updating the 1951 defense agreement for expanded US military access and barring Chinese/Russian minerals exploitation, no sovereignty-transferring pact has materialized. Recent US military expansion bids in April face ongoing resistance, leaving scant time for formal signing amid diplomatic entanglements.

markets.question: Game 2: Any Player Penta Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Penta Kill during Game 2. In League of Legends, a Penta Kill occurs when a single player kills all 5 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Penta Kill during Game 2. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Penta Kill.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Penta Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Penta Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: HMBLE vs Colossal Gaming (BO5) - LIT Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL Upper bracket final match between HMBLE and Colossal Gaming in the LIT Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 7 at 12:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "HMBLE" if HMBLE win the match against Colossal Gaming.

This market will resolve to "Colossal Gaming" if Colossal Gaming win the match against HMBLE.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: HMBLE enters the LIT 2026 Spring Playoffs upper bracket semifinal BO5 against Colossal Gaming as trader consensus favorite, bolstered by their dominant 6-1 regular season record for 1st place and a 3-0 sweep of Colossal in the prior Winter grand final. Both squads advanced convincingly last week with 3-0 playoff wins—HMBLE dismantling GMBLERS Esports on April 23 via superior macro and objective control, Colossal overpowering EKO Esports on April 24. Full rosters intact, HMBLE edges in solo queue rankings with multiple Grandmasters like mid Vigil. In this double-elimination bracket, Colossal's resilient 5-2 regular season form keeps upset potential alive on Patch 26.09.

A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

markets.question: Will the US acquire part of Greenland in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the United States acquires control of any land territory that is part of Greenland by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Only the transfer of sovereignty, or the acquisition of primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control qualifies.

1. Transfer of Sovereignty: This will qualify if a binding agreement or legal instrument results in a defined area of Greenland coming under the formal sovereignty of the U.S. (e.g., incorporated as a U.S. state, territory, possession, or other U.S. political classification), even if the effective date occurs after the market deadline.

2. Acquisition of Primary or Exclusive Jurisdiction or Control: This will qualify if a binding agreement or legal instrument establishes a defined area in Greenland in which the U.S. has primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control over the territory, such that the ordinary legal authority of Denmark and Greenland do not apply,except by U.S. permission. Such agreements or instruments will qualify even if the effective date occurs after the market deadline. 

3. Use of Force: If the U.S. acquires primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control over a defined area of Greenland through force, this will also qualify. 

An announcement will qualify only if it is accompanied by or consists of a binding agreement or legal instrument  (e.g., enacted legislation, a signed treaty, the signed text of an agreement, or an executive action implementing such an agreement) that unambiguously creates a transfer of sovereignty, or primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control,  even if this transfer or acquisition takes effect after the market deadline. 

Non-binding statements, negotiations, proposals, frameworks, or MOUs will not alone qualify. Basing rights, access agreements, SOFA-type arrangements, COFA-type arrangements, commercial concessions, or other permissions to use land (including leases) will not alone qualify. Any qualifying U.S. jurisdiction or control  in Greenland that existed at market creation will not count as new qualifying control.

Examples of qualifying events include but are not limited to treaty or piece of legislation that makes any portion of Greenland a U.S. territory or possession, even if the handover date for such territory or possession is later); or, a Guantánamo-style arrangement establishing a defined zone in Greenland under exclusive or primary U.S. jurisdiction and control, where Denmark and Greenland’s ordinary legal authority does not apply except by U.S. permission.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States, Denmark, and Greenland; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a low 14% chance of the United States acquiring any part of Greenland by year-end, reflecting firm Danish and Greenlandic opposition to sovereignty transfer amid legal barriers under international law and the 1951 defense agreement. Recent trilateral talks in April progressed on expanded U.S. military access to three additional bases—including for missile warning and potential Golden Dome systems—but explicitly avoided territorial control, with Denmark rejecting outright purchase or annexation despite President Trump's rhetoric and a January "framework" announcement. Proposed congressional bills like H.R.7012 remain stalled, and high estimated costs near $700 billion deter action, leaving diplomatic basing deals as the likely outcome absent unforeseen escalations.

eventMetadata.context_description: Post-2026 NFL Draft, trader consensus favors the Baltimore Ravens at 14% implied probability for 2027 AFC Champion, a narrow lead driven by their elite draft haul bolstering the defense around Lamar Jackson and the March trade acquisition of two-time All-Pro edge rusher Maxx Crosby, propelling them atop ESPN power rankings. Buffalo Bills trail closely at 13% on Josh Allen's MVP-caliber consistency and roster stability, while Kansas City Chiefs sit at 10.5% amid lingering wide receiver questions despite Patrick Mahomes' pedigree. Houston Texans (10.1%) and Los Angeles Chargers (9.5%) reflect rising young cores with C.J. Stroud and Justin Herbert under proven coaching, alongside New England Patriots (9.0%) and Denver Broncos (8.5%) draft boosts, underscoring AFC parity with no clear frontrunner amid balanced paths through tough divisions.

markets.question: Will the Communist Party of the Russian Federation (KPRF) win the third-most seats in the next Russian parliamentary election?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Russia in September 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the third greatest number of seats in the next Russian State Duma election.

If the results are not known definitively by September 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the third-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the State Duma of the Federal Assembly of the Russian Federation.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results reported by Russian government sources such as the Central Election Commission of the Russian Federation.

Note: If a listed party contests the election as part of a jointly listed coalition with one or more parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the listed party will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If two or more listed parties contest the election as a jointly listed coalition, with or without additional parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the party within that coalition that won the most seats in the previous election will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If such a coalition dissolves, each market will again represent its respective party individually.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent State Duma polls from April 2026 reflect trader uncertainty in the tight contest for third place behind United Russia's projected dominance, with New People (NL) at 35% implied probability edging Communist Party (KPRF) at 32% and Liberal Democratic Party (LDPR) at 28.5%. WCIOM surveys show NL surging to 13-17%—its highest yet—bolstered by March leadership approvals positioning it as a fresh alternative amid LDPR's post-Zhirinovsky stagnation and KPRF's stable but aging base around 11%. FOM polls, however, favor LDPR at 13% over NL's 8%, highlighting pollster discrepancies and administrative resource gaps in Russia's mixed proportional-single member district system. Separation could arise from upcoming party primaries, candidate lists, or economic pressures shifting protest votes before the September 18-20 election.

markets.question: Will Argentina’s annual inflation in 2026 be between 30% and 34.9%?
markets.description:
This is a market about Argentinian inflation over the 12-month period ending December 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the National Institute of Statistics and Census (INDEC) of Argentina.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI / IPC) over the 12-month period ending in December 2026 (Variación % interanual Total nacional) according to the monthly INDEC report.

The resolution source for this market will be the INDEC Consumer Price Index report released for December 2026 (https://www.indec.gob.ar/), expected to be released in January 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report by clicking on the “Precios al consumidor” option on the home page of https://www.indec.gob.ar/, and searching the pdf for the figure under “Variación % interanual Total nacional”.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly INDEC CPI (IPC) news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 33.6%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Argentina's 2026 annual inflation in a tight race between the 25-29.9% (32%) and 30-34.9% (31.5%) bins, reflecting March 2026 CPI data showing 3.4% monthly inflation—the highest year-to-date—pushing year-over-year rates to 32.6% amid rising transportation and education costs. President Milei's fiscal austerity has slowed annual inflation from 2025 peaks above 200%, but reacceleration risks from seasonal factors and looser monetary signals have lifted central bank surveys to 29.1% full-year expectations and IMF forecasts to 30.5%. Key differentiators include April CPI (due mid-May) and sustained monthly deceleration below 3%; persistent 3%+ prints favor higher bins, while sub-2.5% trajectory supports the lower range.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Mexico City be 17°C or below on May 8?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Benito Juárez International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 8 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Benito Juárez International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/mx/mexico-city/MMMX.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest forecast guidance from major weather services, including The Weather Channel and Weather Underground, projects Mexico City's high temperature on May 8 at around 28°C (82°F), fueling the 55% market-implied odds for 27°C or higher as traders weigh persistent above-normal warmth against emerging risks. This positioning stems from early May 2026 observations exceeding 30°C during a fading heatwave, with the month's mean temperature already +4.7°C above the 19.6°C climatological norm, driven by a stubborn upper-level ridge promoting subsidence and clear morning skies at the city's 2,240-meter elevation. However, 60% odds of afternoon scattered thunderstorms introduce uncertainty, potentially capping peaks and boosting support for 25–26°C outcomes (33% combined). Daily Servicio Meteorológico Nacional updates will provide critical refinements before resolution.

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's recent directive for the Pentagon to withdraw 5,000 US troops from Germany over the next 6-12 months—announced May 2—has heightened NATO tensions, prompting alliance officials to assess impacts on collective defense and transatlantic unity amid ongoing burden-sharing disputes. This repositioning fulfills campaign pledges but stops short of full withdrawal, with no official White House plans for exiting the treaty. A 2023 congressional law bars unilateral presidential termination of US NATO membership, requiring Senate ratification under Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty, erecting significant legal barriers. Traders price low odds reflecting these hurdles, historical precedent against abrupt exits, and focus on diplomatic signals like recent Trump-Rutte discussions, with NATO summits potentially clarifying alliance commitments later in 2026.

Vox's 13-20 seat gains and left fragmentation (Por Andalucía, Adelante Andalucía) constrain PSOE upside, while 24-26 seats (29.5%) captures downside risk in this former socialist stronghold amid national PSOE headwinds. Final campaign pushes and turnout could tip ranges before resolution.

markets.question: Will Joe Flacco start Week 1 for the Chiefs in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who is the starting quarterback for the Kansas City Chiefs in their Week 1 regular season game of the 2026-2027 NFL season.

If the Chiefs do not name a single player as the starting quarterback, the market will resolve based on the player who takes the most snaps at quarterback during their first offensive drive of the game. If multiple players take the same number of snaps on the Chiefs’ first offensive drive, the market will resolve to the player who took the first snap at quarterback.

This market may not resolve before the start of the Chiefs’ Week 1 regular season game. 

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the NFL (NFL.com); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will Trump say "Beach" or "Pool" during events with Brazilian president?
markets.description:
Donald Trump is scheduled to meet with Brazilian President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva on May 7, 2026 (https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/5/4/brazils-lula-to-travel-to-us-to-meet-with-trump-later-this-week). 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump says the listed term during his appearance at events featuring the Brazilian President on May 7, 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

This market is explicitly about events featuring both Donald Trump and Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva on May 7, 2026. All such events featuring both on this date count. Speeches, events, or comments which feature one named individual but not the other, will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the events contain a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

If this visit is definitively cancelled, or otherwise is not aired by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will Jean Castex be on the ballot for the 2027 French presidential election?
markets.description:
The next French presidential election is currently expected to be held in April 2027. Prior to the election, the French Constitutional Council is expected to publish the official list of candidates to be included on the ballot for the first round of this election.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed individual is included on the official candidate list for the first round of the 2027 French presidential election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If no such candidate list is published prior to the election, this market will resolve based on which candidates actually appear on the ballot when the election takes place. If no list is published and the specified election does not take place by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official candidate list published by the French Constitutional Council; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

If no list is published and the specified election does not take place by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official candidate list published by the French Constitutional Council; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: With incumbent Emmanuel Macron ineligible due to term limits, the 2027 French presidential election—first round slated for April 11 or 18—features an unprecedented field amid post-2024 snap legislative elections' hung parliament and government instability. Hard-left La France Insoumise leader Jean-Luc Mélenchon declared his fourth candidacy on May 3, while Les Républicains selected Bruno Retailleau as nominee on April 19 via 74% member vote; others like Édouard Philippe (Horizons) and David Lisnard (LR) have also announced. Polls show National Rally's Jordan Bardella leading first-round hypotheticals at 35-38%, but Marine Le Pen's ineligibility appeal decision on July 7 could shift RN plans. Ballot access requires 500 parrainages from elected officials across 30 departments, posing barriers for fringe candidates; a left-wing primary is set for October 11.

markets.question: T20 World Cup, East Asia-Pacific Qualifier, Regional Final: Philippines vs Indonesia - Toss Match Double Philippines Winner
markets.description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Philippines and Indonesia scheduled for 2026-05-07 in T20 World Cup, East Asia-Pacific Qualifier, Regional Final.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Philippines will be considered correct if  Philippines is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Indonesia will be considered correct if Indonesia is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices the toss at even 50% implied probabilities for Philippines, Indonesia, and draw in this Group 2 opener of the ICC Men's T20 World Cup East Asia-Pacific Qualifier 2026 at neutral Sano International Cricket Ground, Japan, on May 8, underscoring the coin flip's randomness with no captain or venue bias evident. Philippines enter with strong recent form, winning four of their last five T20Is against Indonesia—including a dominant 78-run victory on December 11, 2025—and boasting a W L W W W W streak, while Indonesia slumped to a loss against Malaysia on May 1 amid an L L W L L L run. Absent pitch reports or weather updates, teams lack clear preference for batting or bowling first, keeping the pre-match ritual tightly contested.

markets.question: Will Gold (GC) hit (HIGH) $5,700 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Gold (GC) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Gold (GC) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (February, April, June, August, October, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month changes automatically on the contract's First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Gold (GC) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gold (GC) futures hover around $4,715 for June contracts, with trader sentiment buoyed by persistent central bank purchases—244 tonnes in Q1 2026 extending a 23-month buying streak—and a softer U.S. dollar amid reduced Federal Reserve rate hike bets. March CPI inflation accelerated to 3.3% year-over-year, the highest since May 2024, reinforcing gold's inflation-hedge appeal despite 10-year Treasury yields near 4.35% elevating its opportunity cost. Recent gains past $4,700 reflect these dynamics, tempered by Middle East tensions stoking price pressures. Traders eye April CPI on May 12 and the June 16-17 FOMC meeting as pivotal for rate path revisions impacting gold's trajectory by month-end.

markets.question: Will Africa win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the continent of the country that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup, currently scheduled for June 11-July 19, 2026.

For example, if France wins the tournament, the market will resolve to Europe.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The definitive source of the continent for each country will be World Population Review (https://worldpopulationreview.com/country-rankings/list-of-countries-by-continent).

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/) and the World Population Review; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Europe at 71.5% implied probability to claim the 2026 FIFA World Cup title, reflecting UEFA's 16 qualifiers including top seeds Spain, France, England, Germany, Portugal, Netherlands, and playoff successes like Czechia, Sweden, Bosnia & Herzegovina, and Turkey—bolstering an unmatched talent depth from recent Nations League and Euro campaigns. South America holds steady at 20.5% on CONMEBOL standouts Argentina (defending champions) and Brazil navigating tight qualifiers to secure their six direct spots. The late March intercontinental and UEFA playoffs finalized the 48-team field without seismic shifts, leaving Africa (nine slots via CAF, led by Morocco, Senegal), Asia (eight AFC entrants like Japan, South Korea), North America (CONCACAF hosts USA, Canada, Mexico), and Oceania (New Zealand) as underdogs lacking knockout pedigree despite expanded berths.

markets.question: Will Matteo Berrettini be the 2026 Men’s Wimbledon winner?
markets.description:
Wimbledon 2026 is scheduled for June 29 - July 12, 2026.

This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 Wimbledon Men’s Singles Tournament.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 Wimbledon Men’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 Wimbledon Men’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after August 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jannik Sinner's commanding world No. 1 status, bolstered by back-to-back Masters 1000 triumphs at Monte-Carlo and Madrid in late April—capping a 30-2 record this year—drives trader consensus at 51% implied probability for the 2026 Wimbledon men's title, reflecting his grass-court upside shown in prior Halle wins and overall dominance. Carlos Alcaraz sits second at 18.5% as a proven grass master with consecutive Wimbledon defenses and an Australian Open crown, though his Monte-Carlo final defeat to Sinner tempers enthusiasm. Novak Djokovic's 9.7% share acknowledges seven All England Club victories amid a subdued clay swing and age 39 considerations. Brit Jack Draper and American Taylor Fritz at 3.5% and 2.1% benefit from strong grass histories and potential home-crowd boosts, with French Open outcomes poised to refine pre-grass positioning.

markets.question: LoL: Weibo Gaming vs Ninjas in Pyjamas (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Weibo Gaming and Ninjas in Pyjamas in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for May 10 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Weibo Gaming" if Weibo Gaming win the match against Ninjas in Pyjamas.

This market will resolve to "Ninjas in Pyjamas" if Ninjas in Pyjamas win the match against Weibo Gaming.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: In LPL 2026 Split 2 Group Ascend double round-robin, Ninjas in Pyjamas hold a mid-table 4-3 record (9-7 games) after a 2-1 win over Weibo Gaming on April 9, but recent 0-2 losses to Anyone's Legend and JD Gaming have cooled momentum. Weibo Gaming sit at 3-6 (9-13 games) in the lower half, buoyed by a statement 2-0 upset over top-ranked Top Esports on April 28 yet stumbling 1-2 to Invictus Gaming on May 1. With top-4 securing direct playoffs and bottom-4 risking play-in, this May 10 BO3 rematch under hard fearless draft hinges on WBG's veteran mid Xiaohu and NIP's bot lane edge, amid Patch 26.08 meta favoring aggressive skirmishes.

markets.question: Will Pierre Gasly win the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on the winner of the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix, scheduled for May 24, 2026.

If the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix is canceled or rescheduled to a date after May 31, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve in favor of the driver who is officially listed in first place in the Final Classification published by the FIA following the conclusion of the race. The Final Classification is typically released 30-60 minutes after the race ends and includes any applied time penalties and official adjustments.

Disqualifications or changes made after the publication of the Final Classification will not affect market resolution. The timing of the podium ceremony does not determine the result for this market — only the FIA's published classification will be used to resolve this market.

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and a consensus of credible sports news reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes teammates Kimi Antonelli and George Russell dominate trader consensus for the Canadian Grand Prix driver winner market, with Antonelli's 32% implied probability edging Russell's 28% after the Italian rookie's record-breaking third straight victory in Miami on May 3, extending his championship lead to 20 points. Antonelli's momentum from China, Japan, and Miami wins highlights Mercedes' superior race pace and tire management on varied circuits like Circuit Gilles-Villeneuve, but Russell's experience counters with strong qualifying pedigree and a recent Australian GP triumph. Lando Norris at 17.5% reflects McLaren's competitive straight-line speed, keeping the top tightly bunched amid no major injury concerns or confirmed practice disruptions, underscoring the intra-team battle and potential for qualifying surprises to dictate the podium.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Atlanta be between 72-73°F on May 9?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 9 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ga/atlanta/KATL.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors a high temperature of 72-73°F in Atlanta on May 9 at 50% implied probability, aligned with the latest National Weather Service forecasts showing highs near 71-74°F under mostly cloudy skies. This positioning stems from widespread thunderstorms expected tonight into Thursday morning, ushering in a cooler post-frontal air mass with northeast winds limiting daytime heating after today's highs near 82°F. Model consensus from NOAA ensembles indicates subdued solar insolation and lingering cloud cover, tempering typical May averages around 80°F. Lower outcomes like 63°F or below carry just 3.5% odds due to insufficient evidence for deeper cold pools. Updated guidance arrives overnight, potentially refining trajectories amid inherent short-term forecast uncertainty.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the MD-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the MD-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Democrat April McClain Delaney's strong position in the Cook-rated Solid Democratic MD-06 underpins trader consensus at 87.5% for the Democratic Party, bolstered by her 53.1% 2024 general election win and the district's partisan lean blending Republican-leaning Western Maryland counties with Democratic Montgomery County suburbs. The June 23 primaries loom, with a crowded Democratic field led by Delaney—backed by Gov. Wes Moore and the state delegation—and self-funded challenger David Trone, whose recent attacks on her Laken Riley Act vote prompted a May 5 cease-and-desist amid escalating rhetoric. Modest Republican fundraising signals limited general election threat, sustaining high Democratic implied probabilities absent major shifts.

markets.question: Another US debt downgrade before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States' long-term sovereign credit letter rating is downgraded by any of the three major credit rating agencies (S&P, Moody's, Fitch) at any point by December 31, 2026 11:59pm ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Standard & Poor's, Moody's, or Fitch, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 74.5% implied probability against another major US sovereign credit rating downgrade before 2027, reflecting Fitch Ratings' April 30, 2026 affirmation of its AA+ stable outlook despite projecting US debt-to-GDP at 122% by year-end—up from 116.6% in 2025—amid widening fiscal deficits averaging $1.9 trillion annually per CBO estimates. This stability follows Moody's Aa1 downgrade in May 2025 and prior S&P/Fitch actions, with agencies citing chronic governance gridlock but no acute triggers like debt ceiling default risks, as the $41.1 trillion limit provides headroom above current $39 trillion levels. Key catalysts include FY2027 budget talks and potential debt limit hikes, where bipartisan resolutions have historically averted crises, underscoring the resilience of US Treasuries backed by global reserve demand.

markets.question: Map Handicap: Lavked (-1.5) vs MOUZ NXT (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 3 match between MOUZ NXT and Lavked in the CCT Europe Series 1 Group Stage, initially scheduled for May 7 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Lavked" if Lavked wins 2 or more maps than MOUZ NXT in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "MOUZ NXT". 

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NM-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NM-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Map Handicap: MOUZ (-1.5) vs Gentle Mates (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 1 match between MOUZ and Gentle Mates in the PGL Astana Group Stage, initially scheduled for May 9 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "MOUZ" if MOUZ wins 2 or more maps than Gentle Mates in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Gentle Mates". 

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Will Netflix, Inc. (NFLX) hit (HIGH) $95 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Netflix, Inc. (NFLX) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Netflix, Inc. (NFLX) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.NFLX%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.NFLX%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries holds a commanding lead in the solidly Democratic NY-08 district (Cook PVI D+24), driving trader consensus to 92% for the Democratic Party ahead of the June 23 primaries and November 3 general election. Jeffries' incumbency since 2012, massive fundraising ($12 million raised, $5 million cash on hand as of March), and national profile outweigh challenger Vance Bostic in the Democratic primary, following Chi Ossé's withdrawal in December 2025. Weak Republican primary field—Lewis Mizrahi and Richard Simmons—lacks resources or name recognition in this urban Brooklyn-Queens battleground. Ratings from Cook (Solid D) and Sabato (Safe D) reinforce the status quo, with historical incumbent re-election rates near 95% in such seats. Realistic challenges include a surprise primary upset, late scandal, or extraordinary national midterm wave, though none appear imminent.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump sign an executive order on May 7, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve "Yes" if Donald Trump signs an executive order on the specified calendar date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only formal executive orders will qualify; other executive actions will not qualify.

This market will immediately resolve "Yes" if the text of an executive order for the given day is published on the White House page for presidential actions (https://www.whitehouse.gov/presidential-actions/) or the White House press pool. Mere announcements will not qualify.

If no executive order is published by 12:00 PM ET the day after the listed date of this market, this market will resolve to “No”.

In the case of ambiguity this market may remain open until it is confirmed by the federal register.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's frequent use of executive orders since his January 2025 inauguration—258 signed by May 1, 2026—drives trader consensus toward high YES probabilities (80-96%) for most remaining May dates in this daily market, reflecting expectations of continued action on priorities like retirement security, regulatory reform, and foreign policy sanctions. Recent catalysts include the April 30 executive order establishing TrumpIRA.gov to expand low-cost retirement accounts with federal matching, and the May 1 order imposing sanctions on Cuban regime officials for repression. No formal orders published since May 1 per the White House presidential actions page, anchoring low odds (near 0%) for May 3 and moderated pricing for early May weekdays; resolution hinges on publication by noon ET the following day or Federal Register confirmation, with traders watching for announcements amid ongoing policy implementation.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the TX-15 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the TX-15 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus slightly favors Democrats at 56.5% over Republicans at 46.5% in the TX-15 House race, driven by an April 23 Global Strategy Group poll showing Democratic nominee Bobby Pulido leading incumbent Monica De La Cruz by two points among Latino voters in this Rio Grande Valley battleground district. Pulido's landslide March primary win has unified Democrats, bolstered by national targeting and endorsements like CHC BOLD PAC amid economic concerns—inflation and costs topping voter priorities. The contest stays tight due to De La Cruz's incumbency advantage, fundraising edge, and GOP base in a district rated Likely Republican pre-primary. Hispanic turnout, debates, ad spending, and midterm backlash against President Trump's policies could create separation before the November 3 general election.

markets.question: Will Zelenskyy post 40-59 posts from May 5 to May 12, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Volodymyr Zelenskyy (@ZelenskyyUa), posts on X between May 5, 12:00 PM ET and May 12, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects tightly clustered probabilities across Zelenskyy’s projected X post count for May 5-12, with <20 and 40-59 each at 27% amid recent weeks' output of 64 posts (April 17-24) and 60-79 (April 24-May 1), per resolved markets. This even spread stems from steady communication patterns driven by nightly Russian drone strikes on civilian and energy infrastructure, long-range sanctions updates, and May 1 army reform announcement emphasizing pay hikes and infantry contracts. Absent major catalysts like frontline escalations, diplomatic summits, or NATO summits, volumes hover mid-range; intensified conflict or international endorsements could spike toward 100+, while de-escalation signals might drop below 40.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve maintained its federal funds target range at 3.50%-3.75% following the April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting, despite March CPI surging 3.3% year-over-year—the hottest print since May 2024—and unemployment easing to 4.3% with 178,000 nonfarm payroll gains, underscoring a resilient labor market amid persistent inflation. The March dot plot median projects a 3.4% fed funds rate by end-2026, signaling trader consensus for gradual easing if disinflation resumes. Futures markets price limited near-term cuts, reflecting caution over upside inflation risks. Watch April CPI on May 12 and the June 16-17 FOMC for pivotal shifts in monetary policy expectations before 2027.

markets.question: Will Algeria vs. Austria end in a draw?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for June 27, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Algeria vs. Austria
events.description: This event is for the upcoming FIFA World Cup game, scheduled for Saturday, June 27, 2026 between Algeria and Austria.

eventMetadata.context_description: Austria holds a slim edge in trader consensus at 43% implied probability for victory over Algeria (37.5%) in this pivotal 2026 FIFA World Cup Group J finale at neutral Arrowhead Stadium in Kansas City, with a draw at 29.5% underscoring the tight contest between evenly ranked sides (Austria 24th, Algeria 28th FIFA). Algeria's mounting injury crisis—goalkeepers Anthony Mandrea (shoulder surgery) and Melvin Mastil (groin hernia) sidelined, plus Yacine Titraoui (hamstring) and Amine Gouiri (muscle)—has eroded squad depth just weeks from kickoff, tempering their strong African qualifying campaign. Austria, buoyed by recent friendlies wins over Ghana (5-1) and South Korea (1-0) in late March, leverages superior recent form and defensive solidity despite their own concerns like Maximilian Wöber's muscular issues, keeping probabilities bunched in this high-stakes neutral-venue showdown absent recent head-to-head history.

markets.question: Will Liverpool finish in the top 4 of the EPL 2025–26 standings?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket to predict which clubs finish in the top 4 of the English Premier League standings for the 2025–26 season.

If the listed club officially finishes in the top 4 of the English Premier League standings at the end of the season, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

If two or more clubs are tied on points for a top 4 position, resolution will follow the official English Premier League tiebreaking procedures.

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed before October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal command the Premier League table with 76 points from 35 games, having clinched Champions League qualification, while Manchester City trail closely on 71 from 34 amid their game in hand. Manchester United's 64 points from 35 secure a firm third, bolstered by matchweek 35 results that extended their six-point lead over the fourth-place tussle. Liverpool edge Aston Villa for the final top-four spot, both on 58 points but separated by superior goal difference (+12 vs +4), reflecting Liverpool's recent resilience despite inconsistent form. With three matchweeks left—including Liverpool vs Chelsea in gameweek 36 and potential head-to-heads—fixture difficulty, home advantage, and injury updates will dictate trader consensus on Champions League berths.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by NBA rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Shai Gilgeous-Alexander at 71.5% implied probability for Western Conference Finals MVP, driven by Oklahoma City Thunder's defending champion status, top seed dominance, and 1-0 conference semifinals lead over the Lakers following a 108-90 Game 1 rout where Chet Holmgren posted 24 points and 12 rebounds despite Shai's uncharacteristically sloppy outing. Victor Wembanyama's 20.5% reflects San Antonio Spurs' edge in their tied series versus Minnesota Timberwolves after a 133-95 Game 2 blowout, positioning the young star as Spurs' offensive engine. Lower probabilities for Julius Randle (10.9%, Timberwolves), De'Aaron Fox (10.8%, Kings), and Anthony Edwards (6.4%) hinge on unlikely upsets in ongoing semifinals, underscoring OKC-Spurs as the market's expected matchup amid no major injury reports.

markets.question: Will New Slovenia – Christian Democrats (NSi) be part of the next Government of Slovenia?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed political party is included in the first Slovenian government formed after the 2026 Slovenian parliamentary elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following a successful confirmation vote in the Slovenian National Assembly (Državni zbor), with ministers sworn in under non-caretaker circumstances.

A party will only be considered part of the coalition if it signs the coalition agreement and provides at least one cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government (e.g., through confidence-and-supply arrangements, parliamentary support agreements, or similar) without signing the coalition agreement and holding a cabinet post will not qualify.

If no government is formed, or the results are not known definitively by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the Government of Slovenia.

events.title: Which parties will be part of the next Government of Slovenia?
events.description:

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed political party is included in the first Slovenian government formed after the 2026 Slovenian parliamentary elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following a successful vote of confidence in the Slovenian National Assembly (Državni zbor), with ministers sworn in under non-caretaker circumstances.

A party will only be considered part of the coalition if it signs the coalition agreement and provides at least one cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government (e.g., through confidence-and-supply arrangements, parliamentary support agreements, or similar) without signing the coalition agreement and holding a cabinet post will not qualify.

If no government is formed, or the results are not known definitively by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the Government of Slovenia.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Slovenia's March 22, 2026, parliamentary elections, which produced a hung parliament in the 90-seat National Assembly, coalition negotiations have defined the path to the next government requiring 46 seats for a majority. Incumbent Prime Minister Robert Golob's center-left Freedom Movement secured 29 seats but failed to build a national unity coalition excluding the right-wing Slovenian Democratic Party (28 seats), prompting President Nataša Pirc Musar to skip a prime minister nomination on April 25 after consultations revealed insufficient support. In the ongoing second round, SDS leader Janez Janša is leading center-right talks with Democrats (6 seats) and the NSi-SLS-Fokus alliance (9 seats), with coalition guidelines agreed as of May 5 and details expected soon, potentially relying on Resni.ca (5 seats) for external confidence-and-supply backing ahead of a pivotal confidence vote.

markets.question: Will Kim Jong Un be the next leader out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the first individual who ceases to occupy their listed office.

An announcement of a resignation/removal, or a scheduled departure from office due to the outcome of an election, will not alone qualify.

Only permanent removal from office will qualify for resolution. Temporary removals, such as impeachment suspensions (e.g., Yoon Suk Yeol's recent impeachment), temporary invocation of the 25th Amendment, or any similar provisional transfers of power, will not count.

Additionally, if an individual continues in a caretaker or interim role (e.g., Gabriel Attal remaining as caretaker Prime Minister of France), they will not be considered to have ceased occupying the office for the purposes of this market.

If this criterion has not been met for any of the listed individuals by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “None before 2027”. No additional individuals will be added to this market after its creation.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Over” if more than 5.5 games are played during the 2026 NBA Playoffs Conference Semifinals series between the San Antonio Spurs and Minnesota Timberwolves. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “Under”.

If a partial series is played and not completed by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve according to the number of games played.

If the 2026 NBA Playoffs are cancelled, postponed after May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or the total number of games cannot be determined within that timeframe, this market will resolve to 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the NBA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: NBA Playoffs: Spurs vs. Timberwolves Total Games O/U 5.5

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Over 5.5 games at 62% implied probability for the Spurs-Timberwolves Western Conference semifinals, driven by Anthony Edwards' questionable status for Game 1 due to a left knee hyperextension and bone bruise from the prior round, alongside Ayo Dosunmu's confirmed out (calf). Victor Wembanyama's full health sets up a marquee frontcourt clash with Rudy Gobert, projecting defensive grinds and paint battles that historically extend playoff series. San Antonio's upstart surge meets Minnesota's resilience despite injuries, with both teams' elite defenses (top-5 regular season) and recent first-round advances signaling adjustments over multiple games rather than a quick resolution, amplifying upset potential in a competitive matchup.

markets.question: Anysphere (Cursor) IPO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed company completes an Initial Public Offering (IPO) by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, as confirmed by official company announcements or credible news sources. The IPO refers to the first sale of stock by the listed company to the public on any recognized stock exchange.

If the listed company merges with another entity, is acquired, or ceases to exist before the market resolves, the market will also resolve to "No".

This market will resolve early if the listed company completes an IPO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

The resolution source for this market is a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for major tech IPOs before 2027 centers on high expectations for AI powerhouses OpenAI, Anthropic, and SpaceX, with private valuations exceeding $1 trillion each and reports targeting late-2026 listings amid a resurgent public market that raised $30 billion in U.S. IPOs through Q3 2025. Recent catalysts include Cerebras Systems launching its IPO roadshow for a $26.6 billion AI chip valuation and Canva advancing plans despite an $800,000 regulatory fine for late filings, while Chinese AI firms like Zhipu AI debuted strongly in Hong Kong. Tempering factors: Stripe's delays, Databricks' debt financing preference, and OpenAI CFO signaling a 2027 timeline. Key watchpoints are Q3 S-1 filings and roadshows, with competitive pressures from VC liquidity needs potentially accelerating timelines despite execution risks like profitability scrutiny.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 1200-1239 tweets in May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X during the month of May 2026.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket clusters implied probabilities around 800-919 tweets for Elon Musk's May 2026 X output, reflecting his recent cadence of 24-30 posts per day amid steady engagement on SpaceX milestones like Starlink's Gibraltar rollout, Tesla production updates, and political commentary. Early May data shows 143 posts through day six (averaging 24 daily), projecting a baseline of ~740 but leaving room for upticks from viral threads or xAI announcements, keeping the 800s narrowly ahead. With no dominant frontrunner above 10%, the tight race underscores volatility from daily swings, historical March highs near 1,300, and unpredictable catalysts like government interactions or platform tweaks through May 31.

markets.question: Will Gainare Tottori win on 2026-04-05?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 5, 2026
If Gainare Tottori wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Gainare Tottori vs. Reilac Shiga
events.description: This event is for the upcoming J2 100 Year Vision League game, scheduled for Sunday, April 5, 2026 between Gainare Tottori and Reilac Shiga.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gainare Tottori holds a slim edge as trader consensus favorite at home in the J2/J3 100 Year Vision League West B following their commanding 3-1 away win over Reilac Shiga on April 29, snapping a prior losing streak and highlighting defensive vulnerabilities in Shiga's backline during recent matches like a 2-0 loss to Sagan Tosu. Tottori's momentum from that head-to-head result, combined with Axis Bird Stadium home advantage, drives their 46.5% implied probability, while Shiga's mid-table position (7th) and struggles to keep clean sheets keep their away win viable at 37.5%. The tight odds reflect a closely contested matchup, with draw pricing underscoring frequent stalemates in prior encounters and both teams' inconsistent form. No major injury updates alter lineups.

markets.question: Santos FC vs. Red Bull Bragantino: Both Teams to Score
markets.description:
In the upcoming Brazil Série A game between Santos FC and Red Bull Bragantino, scheduled for May 10 at 5:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Santos FC and Red Bull Bragantino each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on cbf.com.br. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Santos FC vs. Red Bull Bragantino - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Brazil Série A game, scheduled for May 10 at 5:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Santos FC, languishing in 18th place in the 2026 Brasileirão Série A after a dismal 1-5-5 start with just 8 points and a -7 goal difference through 11 matches, hosts mid-table Red Bull Bragantino (9th, 4-2-5, 14 points, +1 GD) at Estádio Urbano Caldeira on May 10. Santos desperately needs points in their relegation scrap, bolstered by home advantage and competitive head-to-head history—including a 2-2 draw last September—but faces injury woes with right-back Mayke (knee), midfielder Gabriel Menino (hamstring), and uncertainty over star forward Neymar following March muscle fatigue that sidelined him briefly. Bragantino contends with absences up front, including strikers Eduardo Sasha and Fernando (both ankle), potentially exposing defensive vulnerabilities on the road amid Santos' urgent momentum shift.
